title: LOW: CORS allows any origin (CorsLayer::new().allow_origin(Any))
description: In src/server.rs lines 14-18, the CORS middleware is configured with allow_origin(Any), allow_methods(Any), allow_headers(Any), and expose_headers(Any). This is maximally permissive. While ActivityPub APIs are inherently public and many Mastodon-compatible servers do this, it means any website can make authenticated cross-origin requests to the API endpoints if a user has credentials. For a single-operator server where the API is used by Mastodon-compatible clients, this is standard practice but worth noting. The OAuth token-based auth provides the real access control. File: /home/mark/PROJECT/smallhold/src/server.rs lines 14-18.
close_reason: Duplicate of smallhold-fihu.11 which was filed in a prior audit pass
